key_insights.insight: True success comes from aligning external actions with internal feelings and making choices that resonate with one's authentic self.
key_insights.quote: I know the best moments of my career, point blank, have been when I have followed my instincts, acted on my own heart.

key_insights.insight: Grief is not a linear process and should not be rushed to make others feel comfortable.
key_insights.quote: Speeding up my process of grief because it makes somebody else feel good is great but also not going to be healthy for me.

key_insights.insight: It's important to surround yourself with people who understand and validate your experiences, especially in moments of vulnerability.
key_insights.quote: I needed you to be there for Jess, who's in the dressing room, not worrying about what the people think that are in the audience watching Jessie J. I need you to care about the girl backstage before I even step on the stage.

key_insights.insight: The most meaningful legacy is the positive feeling and impact you leave on others.
key_insights.quote: The biggest legacy that I think he (Jamal) had...was the feeling that he gave people to me that was his legacy.

key_insights.insight: Loss can be a catalyst for demanding more from yourself and others, pushing for a more fulfilling life.
key_insights.quote: Life is too short to sit anywhere other than where you're supposed to be. And if you're sitting at a table where you don't feel like you're being fed... you politely just leave.

key_insights.insight: Health challenges can provide perspective and keep one grounded in their values.
key_insights.quote: My health has just always had my back when my life has gone like this. It's always kind of gone, take a second.

key_insights.insight: Fame can create a sense of being trapped and disconnected from everyday life.
key_insights.quote: I just felt like I couldn't breathe. I felt like everywhere I went, someone was watching me.

key_insights.insight: One's environment significantly shapes identity and behavior.
key_insights.quote: You are a product of your environment.

actionable_advice.advice: Act on your instincts, especially when something doesn't feel right.
actionable_advice.how_to: Pay attention to your gut feeling and don't dismiss it. If a situation or relationship feels off, trust your intuition and make changes accordingly.

actionable_advice.advice: Set boundaries with loved ones regarding negative media or comments.
actionable_advice.how_to: Communicate clearly to friends and family that you don't want to receive links to critical articles or comments. Ask them to focus on supporting you directly.

actionable_advice.advice: Prioritize your emotional well-being over external pressures, even in professional settings.
actionable_advice.how_to: Be willing to take a step back, re-evaluate priorities, and surrender to your pain, even if it means disappointing others or delaying immediate goals.

actionable_advice.advice: Don't define yourself by your challenges.
actionable_advice.how_to: Even though I had a stroke? And I'm like, yeah, I don't even think about it. I don't define, I don't want to define myself.

memorable_stories: Jessie J's father would pretend to be a bear while camping in the backyard.
Jessie J describes waking up in a hospital ward and seeing a young boy praying not to die, which later inspired her song 'Big White Room'.
Jessie J describes the time a stranger approached her on the street after finding out about her miscarriage and told her that she had to talk about it to help other people.
Dave, Jessie J's bodyguard, visited her in the hospital after a surgery and was told that she couldn't have kids.
contrarian_claims: It is necessary to enable yourself to just break open and be miserable and sad and not have a quote at the end of my moment and just go, no, it's shit and I'm broken and it's awful and I'm sad.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Jessie J: I Quit Music, Deleted An Album, Then Changed My Mind
